The Location
Why Section 227

Section 227 sits in the lower bowl along the first-base line, offering a sharp diagonal view of the entire infield and outfield. You're close enough to track pitches and read the play as it develops, without being buried behind a pole or obstructed by the dugout roof.

Our rows put you roughly 15 to 20 rows from the field — close enough to feel the game, far enough back to see the full defensive picture. The sightlines hold up even in a full house.

Tickets in this section come with access to the Home Plate Terrace Club on the 200 level — Rogers Centre's newly renovated premium space directly behind home plate. It's a genuinely different game-day experience from what most people have at the Rogers Centre.

The Perks
Terrace Club Access

The Home Plate Terrace Club is Rogers Centre's fully reimagined premium level, located in Sections 221–227 directly behind home plate on the 200 level. It was recently renovated from the old Clubhouse Seats into an open-concept lounge and outdoor terrace — a much more social, flexible space than what was there before.

Ticketholders get access to an all-inclusive dining experience: elevated menus alongside ballpark classics, live culinary stations (sushi, pizza oven, ice cream bar), and full indoor and outdoor bar service. Padded seats, in-seat service, and no need to leave your spot to get a drink. Reviewers consistently call it one of the best experiences in the building.

The bar — full liquor service and designer fit-out
Seafood station — crab claws and shrimp
Charcuterie and cheese boards
Sushi station — Rogers Terrace
The Outdoor Experience
Open-Air Terrace

Above the club level sits the Rogers Terrace outdoor patio — a rooftop lounge with a full bar, open-air seating, and unobstructed views of the Toronto skyline. The retractable pergola canopy means it works in sun or cloud. A genuinely rare game-day setting in a North American stadium.

The outdoor terrace — open-air bar and lounge
Rogers Terrace outdoor bar with skyline backdrop
Open-air lounge seating and Toronto skyline
Game-day TV on the terrace lounge
The Food
This Is Not Stadium Food

Every game includes full access to live culinary stations — a carving station with prime rib, shrimp skewers, sushi, charcuterie and cheese boards, a pizza oven, fresh salads, and hot mains. Plus ballpark classics if that's what you're after. All included in the ticket price.

Carving station — prime rib
Pizza oven station
Shrimp skewers and sliders
Herb roasted potatoes, pasta, Brussels sprouts
Fresh salad station
Full bar — wine, spirits, cocktails
What's Included
The Full Package
🍽
All-Inclusive Dining
Elevated menus, ballpark classics, and live culinary stations — sushi, pizza oven, ice cream bar — all included with your ticket.
🥂
Full Bar Service
Upscale indoor and outdoor bars. In-seat drink and food service so you never miss a pitch.
🛋
Premium Club Lounge
Expanded indoor lounge with comfortable seating and a social open-concept layout. Air-conditioned, accessible before, during, and after the game.
🌿
Outdoor Terrace
An open-air terrace space connected directly to your seating — a genuinely different experience from the standard Rogers Centre concourse.
🎯
Perfect Sightlines
Directly behind home plate on the 200 level. Centered views of the full field, pitch location, and the centre-field scoreboard.
🪑
Padded Seating
Padded seats with cup holders throughout — a step above standard lower bowl. Comfortable for a full nine innings.
💼
Great for Client & Employee Perks
Premium club access, all-inclusive hospitality, and marquee matchups make these tickets a natural fit for client entertainment or rewarding your team. A polished, low-effort way to make an impression.
❄
Retractable Roof
Rain is never a concern. The Rogers Centre roof keeps the game on regardless of weather — no last-minute plan changes.
🚇
Transit Access
Union Station is a 5-minute walk. Easy to get downtown clients in, easy to get everyone home.
Pricing
What to Expect

All tickets are priced at $269.98 per ticket ($539.96 per pair) — face value, in compliance with Ontario's Ticket Sales Act. This reflects the Home Plate Terrace Club all-inclusive experience with no separate food and drink tab on top. For client entertainment, that's a straightforward, predictable number to work with.

Good to Know
Details & FAQs

Payment and transfer process. We accept e-transfer prior to transferring tickets. Once payment is confirmed, tickets are transferred digitally through the MLB Ballpark app — you'll receive a notification to accept within a few minutes.

How does ticket transfer work? We transfer tickets digitally through the MLB Ballpark app. Once we confirm your interest, you'll get a transfer notification to accept — takes about two minutes on your phone.

Are these good for corporate entertainment? Yes — the all-inclusive Terrace Club format means a predictable cost and a genuinely elevated experience. No hunting for food lines, no separate tab. Good for clients, good for staff recognition.

What's your trading policy? We're open to straight swaps for other games in our season schedule. Reach out and we'll work it out.
